verification.world_size_used: 1000x700 (verified via StreetBlockScene.js:29-30 — getWorldSize() returns { width: 1000, height: 700 }). NOT 800x600, NOT 1024x768.

notes.world_size_confirmation: Used 1000x700 per the scene's own getWorldSize() return. All `width / 2` references resolved to 500. All `width - N` resolved against 1000 (e.g. width-60 -> 940). All `height + N` resolved against 700 (e.g. height+10 -> 710). All `height / 2` resolved to 350.

loop_generated_arrays_hoisted_to_json.name: trees
loop_generated_arrays_hoisted_to_json.rationale: Per spec section 8: hoisted purely positional array; iteration loop in code now reads this.layout.trees.

loop_generated_arrays_hoisted_to_json.name: plants
loop_generated_arrays_hoisted_to_json.rationale: Per spec section 8: hoisted the plant-config array. Includes species/label alongside positions because the array entries are tightly coupled (same loop body uses all five fields). Behavior is byte-identical — moving const data into JSON does not change behavior. Each plant's x/y/radius is the load-bearing positional/size data.

positions_left_in_code_with_reasons.code: for (let x = 0; x < width; x += 50) { roadGfx.lineBetween(x, 350, x + 25, 350); }
positions_left_in_code_with_reasons.reason: Loop-generated road dash pattern. Per spec section 8 (loop-generated positions are exempt unless the loop bounds are literals worth capturing). Bounds use width from getWorldSize(); the y=350 literal is the road centerline (matches road.y) and the 50/25 literals control dash spacing/length. The road.y is captured in the layout JSON; the loop body coordinates are derived per-iteration.

positions_left_in_code_with_reasons.source_lines: Various setOrigin(0.5), setStrokeStyle(N, 0xCOLOR), interaction radius (70 for GPS), font sizes ('11px', '28px', etc.), depth values, color literals (0xd4a574 etc.)
positions_left_in_code_with_reasons.reason: Per spec rule: setOrigin args are origin floats (0..1), NOT pixel coordinates. Stroke widths, depths, font sizes, interaction radii (constant 70 for GPS — matches ZuzuGarageScene convention), and color literals are styling/behavior, not layout positions. All left in code.

All function bodies untouched. _showRewardCelebration uses dynamic camera-relative positions (cam.scrollX + cam.width/2, +40 offset) — these are per-frame/dynamic positions, exempt per spec section 7. delayedCall(4000) and tween durations (500ms, 400ms) are temporal, not coordinates.

notes.behavioral_changes: NONE. Zero touches to onInteract bodies, dialog strings, quest conditionals (startQuest/getCurrentStep/advanceQuest), audio calls (audioMgr.playSfx/playStinger), save calls (saveGame), NPC_PLACEMENTS lookup, scene transitions (this.scene.start), camera fade callbacks, plant info data, idle messages, MCP emit, or any conditional logic. Verified by reading the diff line-by-line: every change is either the import, the new preload(), the loadLayout() call, or a literal-to-this.layout.NAME swap.
notes.computed_positions_resolved_to_literals: Every `width / 2` -> 500, `width - 60` -> 940, `height + 10` -> 710, `height / 2` -> 350 was resolved to its concrete literal in the JSON. Original expressions are preserved in `_note` fields for human-roundtrip review.
notes.preserved_runtime_property_assignments: this._ramirezBike (line 286) and this._chenBike (line 308) are kept on the instance — they now read x/y from this.layout.ramirez_bike_icon and this.layout.chen_bike_icon respectively. The advanceFromDialog handler (line 467+) still references these properties to update bike icons after quest completion; preserving the assignments guarantees no behavioral drift. this._ecologyPlants (line 130) is also preserved — same shape as before.
